forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KW25ZK93AJ0CR17C9QJ11RXW
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a880
+project: expect
+title: Expectation loader + scope resolution (path/folder/glob/tag)
+---
+## What
+Discover and load expectations, and resolve a `<scope>` argument to a set of them. Template: `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/validators/loader.rs`.
+
+- New `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/loader.rs`:
+  - `ExpectationLoader` that discovers `**/*.expect.md` across the repo (colocated specs anywhere) plus repo-global specs under `.expect/expectations/`. Skip the usual ignored dirs (reuse the skip-dir set used by code_context/project-detection).
+  - `load_all(repo_root) -> Result<Vec<Expectation>, ExpectError>`.
+  - `resolve_scope(scope: &str | None, repo_root, tag: Option<&str>) -> Vec<Expectation>` implementing the three forms from `ideas/expect.md` §"Scope resolution":
+    1. a specific expectation — path to a `*.expect.md`, or its repo-relative path with extension dropped (`src/checkout/coupon`);
+    2. a folder — every `*.expect.md` under it, recursively;
+    3. a glob — shell-style (`src/**/*pricing*.expect.md`) or `--tag <name>`.
+    With no scope: discover every `**/*.expect.md` (the default CI invocation).
+- Identity is the repo-relative path (ext dropped); dedupe by it.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Given a temp repo with specs at `src/a.expect.md`, `src/checkout/coupon.expect.md`, `.expect/expectations/global.expect.md`: `load_all` finds all three.
+- [ ] `resolve_scope("src/checkout/coupon")` ⇒ exactly the coupon spec (extension-dropped form).
+- [ ] `resolve_scope("src/checkout/")` ⇒ all specs under that folder.
+- [ ] `resolve_scope("src/**/*coupon*.expect.md")` (glob) and `--tag` selection both work; empty scope ⇒ all specs.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/loader.rs` unit tests using a `tempfile` repo fixture for each scope form (path, ext-dropped, folder, glob, tag, none).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect loader` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,18 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw2mfq4pa4kzwxzhdpfvfknb
+  text: |-
+    Implemented via TDD. New crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/loader.rs with `ExpectationLoader::new(repo_root)`, `load_all()`, and `resolve_scope(scope: Option<&str>, tag: Option<&str>)`. load_all does a single walkdir pass skipping the shared swissarmyhammer_project_detection::SKIP_DIRECTORIES set (which does NOT contain .expect, so repo-global specs under .expect/expectations/ are discovered alongside colocated specs). resolve_scope tries the three forms in order: specific spec (file path or ext-dropped repo-relative path) -> folder (recursive) -> glob; None/"" => all; optional tag narrows by frontmatter.tags. Results sorted + deduped by repo-relative identity via parse_file/parse_files using the existing Expectation::parse. EXPECT_EXTENSION made pub(crate) in spec.rs so loader shares the single source of truth. Added deps: swissarmyhammer-project-detection, walkdir, glob. Wired module + ExpectationLoader re-export in lib.rs. Runnable doc example on ExpectationLoader.
+
+    Verification: cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect => 42 passed; cargo test --doc -p swissarmyhammer-expect => 2 passed; cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-expect --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt applied.
+
+    Adversarial double-check (REVISE) surfaced two test-quality issues, both fixed: (1) dedup was only vacuously tested -> replaced with a direct parse_files dedup test feeding the same path twice, plus an honest folder+tag narrowing test; (2) skip-dir test re-typed literals -> now iterates swissarmyhammer_project_detection::SKIP_DIRECTORIES.
+
+    Left in doing for /review.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-26T18:53:43.446181+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KW25ZK93AJ0CR17C9QJ11RXW
 position_column: doing

Results sorted + deduped by repo-relative identity via parse_file/parse_files using the existing Expectation::parse.
